segments.text: This is the word that Jeremiah the prophet spoke to Baruch son of Neriah when he wrote these words on a scroll at the dictation of Jeremiah in the fourth year of Jehoiakim son of Josiah, king of Judah:
This is what the LORD, the God of Israel, says to you, Baruch:
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: You have said, ‘Woe is me because the LORD has added sorrow to my pain! I am worn out with groaning and have found no rest.’
segments.speaker: Baruch

segments.text: Thus Jeremiah was to say to Baruch:
This is what the LORD says: Throughout the land I will demolish what I have built and uproot what I have planted.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: But as for you, do you seek great things for yourself? Stop seeking! For I will bring disaster on every living creature, declares the LORD, but wherever you go, I will grant your life as a spoil of war.
segments.speaker: The LORD
